title: Adapter/aligner maintenance vs host API + standards changes
context: After delivery, the forced-alignment adapter, audio-mastering adapter, and publish adapters must be kept current with upstream aligner updates, ffmpeg API changes, host API or policy changes, and standards evolution (EPUB 3 Media Overlays, WebVTT). Without ongoing maintenance, the pipeline degrades silently. This is an ongoing post-delivery maintenance task that becomes active after M1 adapters are live.
objective: Keep the adapters and pipeline current with upstream changes to the forced aligner, audio tooling (ffmpeg), distribution channel APIs, and EPUB/WebVTT standards; record and resolve breaking changes promptly.
acceptanceCriteria: Any breaking change in the forced aligner, ffmpeg loudness API, or distribution-channel API is detected, documented, and resolved within a reasonable maintenance window.
All adapter tests pass after each maintenance update; pnpm build, pnpm test, and pnpm lint are clean.
The per-work record schema (data-001) and consent record format (data-002) are kept compatible with any schema changes.
EPUB 3 Media Overlays and WebVTT output continues to conform to current standards after updates.
DCO sign-off on all maintenance commits.
resources: PLAN.md (sustainability & maintenance section, adapter/aligner maintenance risk)
output: A series of maintenance PRs (one per maintenance cycle or breaking-change event) keeping adapters, pipeline, and output formats current; each PR includes a change log entry describing the upstream change and the fix applied.
